refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Refining it into sub-FRs focused on monitoring energy use, controlling operations to reduce energy, and optimizing system performance aligns with the energy efficiency KPI and supports verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and comparing to E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy; confirm control actions reduce energy use; demonstrate optimization algorithms improve efficiency.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system parameters and operational strategies to achieve energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and load sensors integrated with telemetry system for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive control algorithms in vehicle control system to reduce energy consumption.
DP3: Use data analytics and feedback loops in NGHE solution to optimize operational parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring (DP1) and control (DP2)
Feedback loop coupling between optimization (DP3) and control (DP2)
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and managed via well-defined telemetry and tasking interfaces; modular separation supports manageable dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor data and reliable telemetry communication.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting vehicle responsiveness.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of data and model accuracy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider further decomposing optimization DP if complexity or coupling increases.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level analytics, optimization, and coordination functions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for energy and operational metrics between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands from NGHE Solution to Vehicle for control adjustments.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes optimization and analytics; interfaces well with Vehicle via Telemetry and Tasking.
Hosts control algorithms and sensors; tightly coupled with NGHE Solution via Telemetry.
Minimal role in energy efficiency functions.
No direct involvement in energy efficiency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing energy efficiency optimization and monitoring in NGHE Solution module reduces diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential latency in telemetry data affecting control responsiveness.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution with multiple responsibilities.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting optimization and monitoring into separate submodules if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck if data volume grows.
Control logic split between Vehicle and NGHE Solution requires robust synchronization.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules appropriately; Teleoperation and Safety Services are not involved.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data analysis and operational testing of control and optimization functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of monitoring and optimization to NGHE Solution and control to Vehicle.

recommended_revisions: Monitor telemetry data load and consider interface scaling.
Plan for modularization of optimization if complexity grows.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments are consistent, verifiable, and architecturally sound with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system parameters and operational strategies to achieve energy efficiency target.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Sufficient computational resources in NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
